items.definition: How far a number is from zero. It is always positive.
items.definitionEs: Qué tan lejos está un número de cero. Siempre es positivo.

examples.why: −4 is 4 units from zero.
Absolute value is never negative.

items.cloze: The distance of a number from zero, always positive, is its ___.

items.definition: Add up the areas of the smaller shapes to get the total.
items.definitionEs: Sumar las áreas de las figuras pequeñas para obtener el total.
items.visual: T-shaped hallway: top rectangle = 30 sq ft, bottom rectangle = 28 sq ft → total = 30 + 28 = 58 sq ft
items.cloze: To find the total area of separate parts, I ___ their areas.

items.definition: Adding the same amount to both parts of a ratio table to make new rows.
items.definitionEs: Sumar la misma cantidad a ambas partes de una tabla de razones para hacer filas nuevas.
items.visual: 2:3 → 4:6 → 6:9 (add 2 and 3 each time)
items.cloze: A pattern where you add the same amount each step is an ___.

items.definition: A math phrase that has at least one letter.
items.definitionEs: Una frase matemática que tiene al menos una letra.
items.visual: 3n + 7 means 'triple a number, then add 7' — it has a variable (n), a coefficient (3), and a constant (7)
items.cloze: A math phrase with variables, numbers, and operations but no equal sign is an ___.

items.definition: Adding zeros at the end of a decimal so numbers line up evenly.
items.definitionEs: Agregar ceros al final de un decimal para que los números se alineen.
items.visual: 15.7 becomes 15.70 so you can subtract 15.70 − 8.36 with aligned place values
items.cloze: Writing extra zeros at the end of a decimal so the places line up is called ___.

items.definition: The point at the top of a pyramid where the sides meet.
items.definitionEs: El punto en la parte de arriba de una pirámide donde se unen los lados.
items.visual: The pointy top of a pyramid — all the slanted edges connect here
items.cloze: The single top point where the triangular faces of a pyramid meet is the ___.

items.definition: How much space is inside a flat shape.
items.definitionEs: Cuánto espacio hay dentro de una figura plana.

items.cloze: The amount of flat space inside a two-dimensional figure is its ___.

items.cloze: The rule that grouping does not change a sum or product, like (a + b) + c = a + (b + c), is the ___.

items.definition: 'At least' means ≥. 'At most' means ≤.
items.definitionEs: 'Al menos' significa ≥. 'Como máximo' significa ≤.

items.definition: One of the two parallel sides of a trapezoid.

items.definitionEs: El otro lado paralelo de un trapecio.

items.cloze: The other parallel side of a trapezoid is ___.

items.definition: The area of the bottom of a solid. Volume = base area × height.
items.definitionEs: El área del fondo de un sólido. Volumen = área de la base × altura.

items.definition: A familiar number you use to guess, like 50% or 1/2.
items.definitionEs: Un número conocido que usas para estimar, como 50% o 1/2.
items.visual: Common benchmarks: 25%, 50%, 75%
items.cloze: A common, easy-to-remember value like 50% or 1/4 used for comparing is a ___.

items.definition: The choice that costs less for each item.
items.definitionEs: La opción que cuesta menos por cada artículo.
items.visual: Pack A: $0.50 per pencil vs Pack B: $0.40 per pencil — Pack B is the better buy
items.cloze: The option with the lower unit rate, costing less per item, is the ___.

items.definition: A graph that shows how data is spread using five key numbers.
items.definitionEs: Una gráfica que muestra cómo se reparten los datos con cinco números clave.
items.visual: A box from Q1 to Q3 with a line at the median, and whiskers from min to max
items.cloze: A graph that shows data with a box and whiskers based on the five-number summary is a ___.

items.definition: A filled-in circle showing a number IS included.
items.definitionEs: Un círculo relleno que muestra que un número SÍ está incluido.

items.cloze: A filled-in circle that shows a value IS included, used for ≤ or ≥, is a ___.

items.definition: A group of numbers that are close together.
items.definitionEs: Un grupo de números que están cerca unos de otros.
items.visual: Scores 18, 19, 20, 21, 22 form a cluster around 20 — they are tightly grouped
items.cloze: A group of data values bunched close together is a ___.

items.definition: The number in front of a letter, like the 3 in 3x.
items.definitionEs: El número frente a una letra, como el 3 en 3x.
items.visual: In 3x, the 3 is the coefficient — if x = 4, then 3x = 3 × 4 = 12
items.cloze: A number multiplied by a variable, like the 5 in 5x, is a ___.

examples.why: It is the number multiplied by the variable n.
n is the variable, not the coefficient.

items.definition: Writing a ratio with two dots between the numbers, like 3:2.
items.definitionEs: Escribir una razón con dos puntos entre los números, como 3:2.
items.visual: 3 to 2 can be written as 3:2
items.cloze: Writing a ratio with a colon, like 3:5, is using ___.

items.definition: To add or subtract terms with the same letter.
items.definitionEs: Sumar o restar términos con la misma letra.
items.visual: 4n + 3n = 7n — add the coefficients (4 + 3 = 7) and keep the variable (n)
items.cloze: To add or subtract like terms into one term is to ___ them.

items.definition: A bottom number that two fractions can share so you can compare them.
items.definitionEs: Un número de abajo que dos fracciones pueden compartir para compararlas.
items.visual: To compare 3/5 and 4/7, use LCD 35: 21/35 vs 20/35
items.cloze: A denominator shared by two fractions so they can be compared is a ___.

items.definition: A number that divides two or more numbers evenly.
items.definitionEs: Un número que divide a dos o más números sin que sobre nada.
items.visual: 8 and 12 both divide evenly by 1, 2, and 4 — those are their common factors
items.cloze: A factor that two or more numbers share is a ___ factor.

items.definition: A number that two or more numbers both go into.
items.definitionEs: Un número al que llegan dos o más números al multiplicar.
items.visual: 12, 24, and 36 are all common multiples of both 3 and 4
items.cloze: A multiple that two or more numbers share is a ___ multiple.

items.definition: You can change the order and get the same answer.
items.definitionEs: Puedes cambiar el orden y obtener la misma respuesta.
items.visual: 3 + 7 = 7 + 3 = 10 both ways; 4 × 5 = 5 × 4 = 20 both ways — order does not matter

examples.why: Changing the order keeps the same sum.
That shows the associative property, which regroups.

items.cloze: The rule that order does not change a sum or product, like a + b = b + a, is the ___.

items.definition: To look at ratios and see which is bigger, smaller, or equal.
items.definitionEs: Mirar razones para ver cuál es mayor, menor o igual.
items.visual: 3:4 vs. 2:5 — which has more of the first ingredient per unit?
items.cloze: To decide which ratio is greater or if they are equal is to ___ them.

items.definition: Looking at two or more amounts to see how they are related.
items.definitionEs: Mirar dos o más cantidades para ver cómo se relacionan.
items.visual: 5 eggs vs. 3 eggs — which recipe uses more?
items.cloze: Showing how two amounts relate to each other is a ___.

items.definition: Made by putting two or more simple shapes together.
items.definitionEs: Formada al juntar dos o más figuras simples.
items.visual: 6 triangles, each with area 15 sq ft, combine to form a hexagon with total area = 6 × 15 = 90 sq ft
items.cloze: Made of two or more shapes combined, a figure is ___.

items.definition: A shape made by putting two or more simple shapes together.
items.definitionEs: Una figura formada al juntar dos o más figuras simples.
items.visual: An L-shaped room = a 10×8 rectangle joined to a 4×5 rectangle; total area = 80 + 20 = 100 sq ft
items.cloze: A shape made of two or more basic shapes combined is a ___.

examples.text: An L-shaped room made of two rectangles
examples.why: It is built from two basic shapes.
One basic shape alone is not composite.

items.definition: A number bigger than 1 that you can divide by more than just 1 and itself.
items.definitionEs: Un número mayor que 1 que se puede dividir entre más números, no solo 1 y sí mismo.
items.visual: 12 = 1 × 12, 2 × 6, 3 × 4 — six factors, so 12 is composite

examples.why: 12 has factors 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, and 12.
15 = 3 × 5, so it has more than two factors.
5 is prime; it has only two factors.
2 is prime; its only factors are 1 and 2.

items.cloze: A whole number greater than 1 that has more than two factors is a ___ number.

items.definition: A number on its own that does not change.
items.definitionEs: Un número solo que no cambia.
items.visual: In 3n + 7, the 7 never changes no matter what n is — like a flat fee that stays the same

examples.why: It is a number with no variable.
It has a variable, so it is not a constant.

items.cloze: A term that is just a number and never changes, like the 5 in 3x + 5, is a ___.

items.definition: A ratio that helps you change one unit into another.
items.definitionEs: Una razón que te ayuda a cambiar una unidad por otra.
items.visual: 1 foot = 12 inches, so the factor is 12
items.cloze: A ratio equal to 1 that relates two units, like 12 inches / 1 foot, is a ___.

items.definition: To change a number to a new form but keep the same value.
items.definitionEs: Cambiar un número a otra forma sin cambiar su valor.
items.visual: 2 1/3 → multiply 2 × 3 = 6, add 1 → 7/3. Same value, different form.
items.cloze: Changing a mixed number into an improper fraction is to ___ it.

items.definition: A grid with a line going across and a line going up to plot points.
items.definitionEs: Una cuadrícula con una línea horizontal y una vertical para marcar puntos.
items.visual: A + shape made by two number lines crossing at the center point (0, 0)
items.cloze: The flat grid formed by a horizontal and a vertical number line is the ___.

items.definition: Multiplying across two ratios to check if they are equal.
items.definitionEs: Multiplicar en cruz dos razones para ver si son iguales.
items.cloze: Multiplying the numerator of one ratio by the denominator of the other to check a proportion is to ___.

items.definition: The units used to measure space inside, like cubic inches.
items.definitionEs: Las unidades para medir el espacio interior, como pulgadas cúbicas.
items.visual: A tiny cube that is 1 in × 1 in × 1 in = 1 in³ (one cubic inch)
items.cloze: The unit used to measure volume, like cubic centimeters, is ___.

items.definition: The U.S. way of measuring, like inches, feet, and pounds.
items.definitionEs: La forma de medir de EE. UU., como pulgadas, pies y libras.
items.visual: 12 inches = 1 foot, 3 feet = 1 yard, 16 ounces = 1 pound

examples.why: A foot is a customary unit of length.
A meter is a metric unit.

items.cloze: Units like inches, feet, and pounds used mainly in the U.S. are ___.

items.definition: Facts and numbers you collect, like answers to a survey.
items.definitionEs: Datos y números que recoges, como respuestas a una encuesta.
items.visual: Player heights: 60 in, 63 in, 58 in, 65 in — four different measurements
items.cloze: Facts or numbers collected to answer a question are ___.

items.definition: How the data looks: where it sits and how spread out it is.
items.definitionEs: Cómo se ven los datos: dónde están y qué tan separados están.

items.cloze: The way data values are spread out across a range is the ___.

items.definition: A number with a dot that shows a part between whole numbers.
items.definitionEs: Un número con un punto que muestra una parte entre números enteros.

items.cloze: A number that uses a point to show a value less than one whole is a ___.

items.definition: Dividing with decimals. First make the number you divide by a whole number.
items.definitionEs: Dividir con decimales. Primero haz entero el número entre el que divides.

items.cloze: Dividing numbers that include a decimal point is called ___.

items.definition: How many digits come after the decimal point.
items.definitionEs: Cuántos dígitos hay después del punto decimal.

items.cloze: To place the point in a product, I count the total number of ___ in both factors.

items.definition: The dot that splits the whole number from the part after it.
items.definitionEs: El punto que separa el número entero de la parte que sigue.
items.visual: In 3.25, the decimal point separates 3 (ones) from 25 (tenths and hundredths)
items.cloze: The dot that separates whole numbers from parts of a whole is the ___.

items.definition: To break a shape into smaller, simpler shapes.
items.definitionEs: Separar una figura en figuras más pequeñas y simples.
items.visual: Draw lines from the center of a hexagon to each corner → you get 6 equal triangles you can find the area of
items.cloze: To break a shape into smaller shapes is to ___ it.

items.definition: How far a number is from the mean.
items.definitionEs: Qué tan lejos está un número de la media.

items.cloze: How far a single value is from the mean is its ___.

items.definition: How long, how wide, and how tall a shape is.
items.definitionEs: Qué tan largo, ancho y alto es una figura.

items.cloze: The measurements of length, width, and height of a solid are its ___.

items.definition: How far apart two points are. It is never negative.
items.definitionEs: Qué tan separados están dos puntos. Nunca es negativo.
items.visual: The distance from -3 to 4 on a number line is |-3| + |4| = 3 + 4 = 7 units
items.cloze: How far apart two points are on the coordinate plane is the ___.

items.definition: How the data is spread out.
items.definitionEs: Cómo están repartidos los datos.
items.visual: Most data in the middle with fewer at the ends = bell-shaped; most on one side with a tail = skewed
items.cloze: The overall pattern of how data is spread out is the ___.

items.definition: Multiplying a number by everything inside the parentheses: a(b + c) = ab + ac.
items.definitionEs: Multiplicar un número por todo lo que está dentro del paréntesis: a(b + c) = ab + ac.

The 3 must also multiply the 4.

items.cloze: The rule that a(b + c) = ab + ac is the ___.

items.definitionEs: Repartir un total en grupos iguales.

items.cloze: To find a unit rate, I ___ the price by the number of units.

items.definition: The number you are splitting up in a division problem.
items.definitionEs: El número que estás repartiendo en una división.
items.visual: In 156 ÷ 12 = 13, the dividend is 156 — it is the total being split up
items.cloze: In 84 ÷ 7, the number 84 being divided is the ___.

items.definition: A number you can divide evenly, with nothing left over.
items.definitionEs: Un número que se puede dividir sin que sobre nada.

examples.why: 15 ÷ 3 = 5 with no remainder.
20 ÷ 4 = 5 with no remainder.
10 ÷ 3 leaves a remainder of 1.

items.definition: The number you split by in a division problem.
items.definitionEs: El número entre el que divides en una división.

items.cloze: In 84 ÷ 7, the number 7 that we divide by is the ___.

items.definition: The line where two flat sides of a solid meet.
items.definitionEs: La línea donde se unen dos lados planos de un sólido.
items.visual: A cube has 12 edges — 4 along the top, 4 along the bottom, 4 vertical
items.cloze: A line segment where two faces of a solid meet is an ___.

items.definition: The = sign, showing both sides are the same.
items.definitionEs: El signo =, que muestra que ambos lados son iguales.

items.cloze: The symbol = that shows two amounts are the same is the ___.

items.definition: A math sentence with an equal sign showing both sides are the same.
items.definitionEs: Una oración matemática con un signo igual que muestra que ambos lados son iguales.
items.visual: 3/4 ÷ 1/8 = 6 — the left side (division) equals the right side (answer)

items.cloze: Two ratios that show the same comparison are ___.

items.definition: Multiplying both numbers by 10 or 100 gives the same answer.
items.definitionEs: Multiplicar ambos números por 10 o 100 da la misma respuesta.
items.visual: 7.56 ÷ 0.12 = 756 ÷ 12 = 63 (both multiplied by 100)
items.cloze: Multiplying both the dividend and divisor by the same power of 10 creates an ___ that is easier to solve.

items.definition: Two ratios that mean the same thing, like 1:2 and 2:4.
items.definitionEs: Dos razones que significan lo mismo, como 1:2 y 2:4.

items.definition: Two ratios that mean the same thing.
items.definitionEs: Dos razones que significan lo mismo.
items.visual: 2:3 and 4:6 both mean 2 out of every 3, so 2/3 = 4/6

items.definition: A close answer you get by rounding first.
items.definitionEs: Una respuesta cercana que obtienes al redondear primero.

items.definition: To find the value of an expression.
items.definitionEs: Encontrar el valor de una expresión.
items.visual: Evaluate 3⁴: write 3 × 3 × 3 × 3, then multiply step by step: 9 × 9 = 81
items.cloze: To find the value of an expression, like 2³ = 8, is to ___ it.

items.definition: To multiply out the parentheses in an expression.
items.definitionEs: Multiplicar lo que está dentro del paréntesis en una expresión.
items.visual: Expand 2(n + 6): multiply 2 × n = 2n and 2 × 6 = 12, so 2(n + 6) = 2n + 12
items.cloze: To multiply out a product over a sum, like 3(x + 4) = 3x + 12, is to ___.

items.definition: A small number that tells how many times to multiply a number by itself.
items.definitionEs: Un número pequeño que dice cuántas veces multiplicar un número por sí mismo.
items.visual: 2³ means 2 × 2 × 2 = 8

examples.why: It tells how many times 2 is multiplied by itself.
The 2 is the base, not the exponent.
It shows 2 is multiplied 4 times.
Here 5 is a factor, not an exponent.

items.cloze: In 2³, the small number 3 is the ___ and it shows 2 is multiplied 3 times.

items.definition: A math phrase with numbers and letters, but no equal sign.
items.definitionEs: Una frase matemática con números y letras, pero sin signo igual.
items.visual: 2n + 7 is an expression: the 2n means '2 times some number' and the + 7 means 'then add 7'

examples.why: It has terms and operations but no equal sign.
That is an equation because it has an equal sign.

items.cloze: A group of numbers, variables, and operations with no equal sign is an ___.

items.definition: One flat side of a solid shape.
items.definitionEs: Un lado plano de una figura sólida.

items.cloze: A flat surface of a solid figure is a ___.

items.definition: A number that divides another number with nothing left over.
items.definitionEs: Un número que divide a otro sin que sobre nada.

examples.why: 12 ÷ 3 = 4 with no remainder.
12 ÷ 4 = 3 with no remainder.
12 ÷ 5 leaves a remainder, so 5 is not a factor of 12.

items.cloze: A number that divides evenly into another number with no remainder is a ___.

items.definition: A picture that splits a number into its prime numbers, step by step.
items.definitionEs: Un dibujo que separa un número en sus números primos, paso a paso.
items.cloze: I can break a number into its prime factors step by step using a ___.

items.definition: A math rule written with symbols.
items.definitionEs: Una regla matemática escrita con símbolos.

items.cloze: A math rule written with symbols, like A = b × h, is a ___.

items.definition: A number that shows part of a whole, like 3/4.

items.definitionEs: Un porcentaje mayor que 100%, más que todo el entero.

items.definition: How far apart two points are going left or right.

items.definition: A math sentence that compares two sides with <, >, ≤, or ≥.

items.definitionEs: Dos operaciones que se deshacen entre sí, como × y ÷.

items.definition: How long, how wide, and how tall a box is.

items.definitionEs: Un porcentaje menor que 1%, una parte muy pequeña.

items.definition: Terms with the same letter, like 2x and 5x.
items.definitionEs: Términos con la misma letra, como 2x y 5x.

items.definitionEs: Dinero que se suma al costo para fijar el precio de venta.

items.cloze: The average found by adding all values and dividing by how many there are is the ___.

items.cloze: The average distance of each value from the mean is the ___.

items.definition: The middle number when you put them in order.
items.definitionEs: El número del medio cuando los pones en orden.

items.cloze: The middle value of a data set when the numbers are in order is the ___.

items.definition: A way of measuring by 10s, like meters, liters, and grams.
items.definitionEs: Una forma de medir de 10 en 10, como metros, litros y gramos.
items.visual: 1 meter = 100 centimeters, 1 kilogram = 1,000 grams

examples.why: A gram is a metric unit of mass.
A pound is a customary unit.

items.cloze: Units like meters, grams, and liters based on tens are ___.

items.definition: A whole number plus a fraction, like 2 1/2.
items.definitionEs: Un número entero más una fracción, como 2 1/2.

items.cloze: A number with a whole number and a fraction, like 2 1/3, is a ___.

items.definitionEs: El número que aparece más veces.

items.cloze: The value that appears most often in a data set is the ___.

items.definition: A picture or math way to show a problem so you can solve it.
items.definitionEs: Un dibujo o forma matemática de mostrar un problema para resolverlo.

items.definition: What you get when you multiply a number by 1, 2, 3, and so on.
items.definitionEs: Lo que obtienes al multiplicar un número por 1, 2, 3, y así.

items.definitionEs: Un número entero menor que cero, como -5.

items.definition: The number you multiply both parts of a ratio by to get an equal ratio.
items.definitionEs: El número por el que multiplicas ambas partes de una razón para obtener una razón igual.
items.visual: 2:3 × 2 → 4:6 (scale factor is 2)
items.cloze: The number you multiply a ratio by to get an equivalent ratio is the ___.

items.definition: To make a fraction smaller using the same parts, like 2/4 = 1/2.
items.definitionEs: Hacer una fracción más pequeña con las mismas partes, como 2/4 = 1/2.
items.cloze: Writing a fraction in its smallest equal form, like 4/8 = 1/2, is to ___ it.

items.definition: When most data sits on one side with a tail on the other.
items.definitionEs: Cuando la mayoría de los datos está de un lado con una cola del otro.
items.visual: Scores: 5, 6, 7, 8, 8, 35 → most scores are low, but 35 creates a tail to the right (skewed right)

examples.text: Most values low with a few very high values
examples.why: The data has a tail, so it is skewed.

examples.text: Values spread evenly around the center
examples.why: That is symmetric, not skewed.

items.cloze: Data that is bunched on one side with a tail on the other is ___.

items.definition: Counting by a number, like 2, 4, 6, to list its multiples.
items.definitionEs: Contar de un número en un número, como 2, 4, 6, para listar sus múltiplos.
items.visual: Skip count by 6: 6, 12, 18, 24, 30 — each jump adds 6
items.cloze: Counting forward by a number other than 1, like 6, 12, 18, is called ___.

items.definition: The height of a side triangle, measured along its slanted face.
items.definitionEs: La altura de un triángulo lateral, medida por su cara inclinada.
items.visual: If you slide your finger from the bottom edge up the triangle face to the top point — that distance is the slant height
items.cloze: The height of a triangular face of a pyramid, measured along the slanted side, is the ___.

items.definition: The answer to an equation or problem.
items.definitionEs: La respuesta a una ecuación o problema.
items.visual: In 3/4 ÷ 1/8 = 6, the solution is 6 portions

usedBy.lessonTitle: Solve One-Step Addition and Subtraction Equations

items.cloze: The value that makes an equation true is the ___.

items.definition: All the numbers that make the inequality true.
items.definitionEs: Todos los números que hacen verdadera la desigualdad.
items.visual: For x > 3, the solution set includes 3.1, 4, 5, 10, 100 — any number greater than 3
items.cloze: All the values that make an inequality true form the ___.

items.definition: To find the number that makes it true.
items.definitionEs: Encontrar el número que la hace verdadera.
items.visual: x + 3 > 10 → subtract 3 → x > 7 — any number greater than 7 works
items.cloze: To find all the values that make an inequality true is to ___ it.

items.definition: How far apart the numbers are.
items.definitionEs: Qué tan separados están los números.
items.visual: Low spread: 8, 9, 10, 11 (close together). High spread: 2, 9, 10, 25 (far apart)
items.cloze: How far apart the data values are from each other is the ___.

items.definition: A question where the answers will be different for different people.
items.definitionEs: Una pregunta cuyas respuestas serán distintas para distintas personas.
items.visual: "How tall are the players on the team?" → answers vary: 60 in, 63 in, 58 in, 65 in

examples.text: How tall are the students in my class?
examples.why: The answers vary, so data must be collected.
It has one fixed answer, so it is not statistical.

items.cloze: A question that expects a variety of answers and is answered by collecting data is a ___.

items.definition: To put a number in place of a letter.
items.definitionEs: Poner un número en lugar de una letra.
items.visual: Substitute 3 for x in 5x: replace x with 3 → 5(3) = 15
items.cloze: To replace a variable with a number is to ___.

items.definition: Take away the area of a missing piece from a bigger shape.
items.definitionEs: Quitar el área de una parte que falta de una figura más grande.
items.visual: A 14×10 pool with a 6×4 cutout: 140 − 24 = 116 sq ft of water surface
items.cloze: To find the area of a shape with a piece removed, I ___ the missing part's area.

items.definition: The total area of all the flat sides of a solid.
items.definitionEs: El área total de todos los lados planos de un sólido.
items.visual: A box 6 × 4 × 2: SA = 2(24) + 2(12) + 2(8) = 88 in² — like wrapping paper needed
items.cloze: The total area of all the faces of a solid is its ___.

items.definition: Collecting facts by asking people questions.
items.definitionEs: Recoger datos haciendo preguntas a las personas.
items.visual: Asking 50 students 'What is your favorite sport?' and recording the answers
items.cloze: A way to collect data by asking many people the same question is a ___.

items.definition: Data that looks about the same on the left and right.
items.definitionEs: Datos que se ven casi iguales a la izquierda y a la derecha.
items.visual: Heights: 60, 62, 64, 66, 68, 66, 64, 62, 60 — rises and falls evenly like a hill

examples.text: Data balanced evenly around the center
examples.why: Both sides match, so it is symmetric.

examples.text: Data with a long tail to the right
examples.why: That is skewed, not symmetric.

items.cloze: A distribution where both sides look like mirror images is ___.

items.definition: When a shape looks the same on both sides of a line.
items.definitionEs: Cuando una figura se ve igual a ambos lados de una línea.
items.visual: A butterfly's wings are symmetric — fold it down the middle and both sides match
items.cloze: When two halves are mirror images of each other, the figure has ___.

items.definition: Extra money added to a price for the government.
items.definitionEs: Dinero extra que se suma a un precio para el gobierno.
items.visual: 6% tax on $20 adds $1.20
items.cloze: An extra percent added to a purchase that goes to the government is ___.

items.definition: The first spot after the decimal point. It shows parts out of 10.
items.definitionEs: El primer lugar después del punto decimal. Muestra partes de 10.
items.visual: 0.7 = 7/10 — like 7 slices of a pizza cut into 10 equal slices

examples.why: 0.3 means three tenths.
That 3 is in the hundredths place.

items.cloze: The first place to the right of the decimal point is the ___ place.

items.definition: Extra money you give for good service.
items.definitionEs: Dinero extra que das por un buen servicio.
items.cloze: Extra money added for good service, often a percent of the bill, is a ___.

items.definition: A four-sided shape with just one pair of parallel sides.
items.definitionEs: Una figura de cuatro lados con solo un par de lados paralelos.
items.visual: A table top wider than the bottom shelf — the top and bottom edges are parallel, the two sides slant inward

examples.text: A four-sided shape with one pair of parallel sides
examples.why: It has exactly one pair of parallel sides.
A rectangle has two pairs of parallel sides.

items.cloze: A quadrilateral with exactly one pair of parallel sides is a ___.

items.definition: A shape with three sides.
items.definitionEs: Una figura de tres lados.
items.visual: Each triangle inside a decomposed hexagon has a base = one side of the hexagon and height = distance from center to side
items.cloze: A three-sided polygon is a ___.

items.definition: A solid with two triangle ends and three flat rectangle sides.
items.definitionEs: Un sólido con dos extremos triangulares y tres lados rectangulares.
items.visual: Shaped like a tent or a wedge of cheese — 2 triangle ends + 3 rectangle sides

examples.text: A tent shape with triangle ends
examples.why: It has two triangular faces and three rectangular faces.
A cube has six square faces, no triangles.

items.cloze: A solid with two triangular faces and three rectangular faces is a ___.

items.definition: A flat shape with length and width, but no thickness.
items.definitionEs: Una figura plana con largo y ancho, pero sin grosor.
items.visual: A rectangle drawn on paper is 2D — it has length and width but no depth
items.cloze: A flat shape with length and width but no thickness is ___.

items.definition: A standard amount used to measure, like an inch or a liter.
items.definitionEs: Una cantidad estándar para medir, como una pulgada o un litro.
items.visual: Inches, feet, pounds, ounces
items.cloze: A standard amount used to measure, like a foot or a gram, is a ___.

items.definition: A fraction with 1 on top, like 1/4.
items.definitionEs: Una fracción con 1 arriba, como 1/4.
items.visual: 1/2, 1/3, 1/4, 1/5 — each represents one equal part of a whole

examples.why: Its numerator is 1.
Its numerator is 3, not 1.

items.cloze: A fraction with a numerator of 1, like 1/5, is a ___.

items.definition: A rate for just 1 of something, like cost for 1 item.
items.definitionEs: Una tasa para solo 1 de algo, como el precio de 1 artículo.
items.visual: $3 per 1 pound → unit rate is $3/lb
items.cloze: A rate that tells the amount for exactly one unit, like 5 miles per 1 hour, is a ___.

examples.why: It gives the amount for exactly one unit.
That is a rate for 3 units, not a unit rate.

items.definition: How spread out the numbers are.
items.definitionEs: Qué tan separados están los números.
items.visual: Scores 10, 12, 11 are close together (low variability); scores 2, 15, 30 are far apart (high variability)
items.cloze: How much the values in a data set differ from one another is the ___.

items.definition: A letter that stands for an unknown number.
items.definitionEs: Una letra que representa un número desconocido.
items.visual: In 3x + 5, x can be any number — if x = 2, the expression equals 11; if x = 10, it equals 35
items.cloze: A letter that stands for an unknown number, like x, is a ___.

items.definition: How far apart two points are going up or down.
items.definitionEs: Qué tan separados están dos puntos yendo hacia arriba o hacia abajo.
items.visual: From (4, -1) to (4, 6): count from -1 to 6 = 7 units up
items.cloze: The distance between two points along the y-direction is the ___.

items.definition: How much space is inside a solid shape.
items.definitionEs: Cuánto espacio hay dentro de una figura sólida.
items.visual: A box 3 × 2 × 4 holds 24 unit cubes, so V = 24 cubic units
items.cloze: The amount of space inside a three-dimensional solid is its ___.

items.definition: A counting number with no fraction or decimal, like 0, 1, 2, 3.
items.definitionEs: Un número de contar sin fracción ni decimal, como 0, 1, 2, 3.
items.visual: 4 is a whole number; as a fraction it is 4/1
items.cloze: A counting number like 0, 1, 2, or 3 with no fraction or decimal part is a ___.

items.definition: The line that goes across the grid. Flipping over it changes the y sign.
items.definitionEs: La línea que va de lado en la cuadrícula. Voltear sobre ella cambia el signo de y.
items.visual: (4, 3) → (4, -3): the y flips from +3 to -3, like folding the paper along the horizontal line
items.cloze: The horizontal number line on the coordinate plane is the ___.

items.definition: The line that goes up the grid. Flipping over it changes the x sign.
items.definitionEs: La línea que va hacia arriba en la cuadrícula. Voltear sobre ella cambia el signo de x.
items.visual: (4, 3) → (-4, 3): the x flips from +4 to -4, like folding the paper along the vertical line
items.cloze: The vertical number line on the coordinate plane is the ___.
